description: Address code review findings: fix double mana regen, fragmented event dispatch, ConcurrentModificationException risk, and Random instantiation per call.
steps: Step 1: Remove regenMana(10) from Player.attack() - the controller owns mana regen timing
Step 2: Fix Random instantiation in Player.performBasicAttack() - hoist to a field
Step 3: Fix Random instantiation in Enemy.attack() - hoist to a field
Step 4: Fix ConcurrentModificationException in GameEventDispatcher.fireEvent() - use snapshot iteration
Step 5: Add central event forwarding in CombatEngine - re-fire child events from player/enemies/subsystems so views only register once
Step 6: Add try/finally in CombatEngine.processPlayerAction() to prevent stuck state on exception
Step 7: Verify compilation: javac -d out $(find src -name '*.java')
acceptance_criteria: Player.attack() does NOT call regenMana() - mana regen is solely the controller's responsibility
Player.performBasicAttack() uses a field-level Random, not a new instance per call
Enemy.attack() uses a field-level Random, not a new instance per call
GameEventDispatcher.fireEvent() is safe against ConcurrentModificationException (snapshot or CopyOnWriteArrayList)
CombatEngine re-fires events from child objects (player, enemies, subsystems) so a view can register on the engine alone
CombatEngine.processPlayerAction() uses try/finally to prevent stuck PROCESSING_ACTION state
Full project compiles with: javac -d out $(find src -name '*.java')
No entry point created - Phase 3 handles that
verification: Run: javac -d out $(find src -name '*.java') and confirm zero compilation errors
Verify Player.attack() has no regenMana call
Verify no 'new Random()' inside method bodies in Player.java or Enemy.java
